docs.name: MÖRK BORG (compatible)
docs.tagline: A doom metal d20 of dying dirt-bags
docs.author: XRPG (third-party content, MÖRK BORG-compatible)
docs.summary: A rules-light, doom-soaked d20 system built for a dying world. Roll 1d20, add the relevant Ability, and try to meet or beat the Difficulty Rating (default DR12). This is original, openly-licensed third-party content compatible with MÖRK BORG — keep the misery, bring your own scrolls.

dice.summary: 1d20 + Ability ≥ DR (default DR12). Natural 20 = critical success, natural 1 = fumble.

attributes.name: Agility
attributes.desc: Dodging, climbing, sneaking, balance, and Defense against incoming attacks.

attributes.name: Presence
attributes.desc: Force of will, command, ranged aim, and the wielding of Powers and unclean Scrolls.

attributes.name: Strength
attributes.desc: Melee attacks, brute force, lifting, breaking, and grappling.

attributes.name: Toughness
attributes.desc: Stamina, resisting poison, disease, and cold; the basis of your Hit Points.

attributeRules.note: Generate each ability by rolling 3d6: 1-4 = -3, 5-6 = -2, 7-8 = -1, 9-12 = ±0, 13-14 = +1, 15-16 = +2, 17-18 = +3. Roll them in order: Agility, Presence, Strength, Toughness.

deriveds.name: Hit Points
deriveds.desc: Roll 1d8 + Toughness at creation (min 1). This shows a representative value; you can edit it after rolling.

deriveds.name: Omens
deriveds.desc: 1d2 fate points per session, spent to reroll, ignore damage, or reduce a Broken result. Refreshes each session.

conditions.name: Broken
conditions.effect: At 0 HP you collapse, Broken. Roll 1d4 on the Broken table: jaw shattered & 1d4 teeth lost (unconscious 1 hour); fall over weeping (paralyzed by pain, unconscious 1d4 rounds); bleeding & dying (die in 1d4 rounds unless tended; thereafter at 1d4 HP); or dead (or as good as).

conditions.name: Bleeding Out
conditions.effect: Dying. You will be dead in 1d4 rounds unless an ally reaches you and succeeds a DR12 Presence test to staunch the wound. Stabilized, you stagger up at 1d4 HP.

conditions.name: Infected
conditions.effect: A festering wound or filthy bite. Each day make a DR12 Toughness test; on a fail lose 1d4 HP and the infection spreads. Three failures means death by rot and fever, then you may not stay still.

conditions.name: Falling Apart
conditions.effect: Cursed, starving, or rotting from within. −2 (DR +2) to all tests and you cannot recover HP through rest until the cause is removed. Often a slow, deserved death.

weapons.name: Femur / improvised
weapons.notes: Anything you can grab. Brutal and pathetic.

weapons.name: Bow
weapons.notes: Ranged. Presence to fire. Needs both hands; ammo is scarce.

weapons.name: Crossbow
weapons.notes: Ranged, hard-hitting; reload takes an action.

weapons.name: Sling / thrown rock
weapons.notes: Ranged. Last resort of the truly poor.

bestiary.name: Pale One
bestiary.statblock:
**HP** 6 · **Morale** 8 · **Defense** DR10

Grey, drowned-looking dead that shamble from cellars and bogs. Cold to the touch and utterly without fear.

- **Grasping hands** (DR12 attack): 1d4 damage. On a hit, the target must pass DR12 Toughness or contract a creeping rot (**Infected**).
- **No pain:** ignores the first point of damage from any single blow.
- **Special:** if reduced to 0 HP but the head is intact, it claws after you for one more round.

bestiary.name: Goblin Scavenger
bestiary.statblock:
**HP** 4 · **Morale** 6 · **Defense** DR13

Spindly, knife-eared scavengers that hunt the dead and the dying in packs.

- **Rusty blade** (DR12 attack): 1d4 damage.
- **Sling** (DR12 ranged): 1d4 damage.
- **Cowardly:** tests Morale the instant one of its pack falls. Fights viciously when cornered or in numbers of five or more.

bestiary.name: Undead Wretch
bestiary.statblock:
**HP** 8 · **Morale** — (never flees) · **Defense** DR11

The restless dead of a damned land — slow, relentless, and never alone.

- **Rotten claws & teeth** (DR12 attack): 1d6 damage.
- **Mindless:** immune to fear, Morale, and any Power that sways the living mind.
- **Slow:** acts last in the round. A nimble victim (DR12 Agility) can outrun a lone Wretch — but rarely a whole churchyard of them.

bestiary.name: Cursed Knight
bestiary.statblock:
**HP** 14 · **Morale** 11 · **Defense** DR15 (heavy, rusted plate)

An oath-bound corpse welded into its armor, still patrolling a keep that fell to ruin centuries ago.

- **Cursed greatsword** (DR12 attack, +2 to hit): 1d10 damage.
- **Armor:** the rusted plate negates 1d4 damage per hit and imposes Hard (DR16) on any attempt to wound it cleanly.
- **Undying:** at 0 HP it crumbles, but on a roll of 1d6 = 1 it reassembles over the next night unless the helm is shattered.

bestiary.name: Catacomb Saint
bestiary.statblock:
**HP** 18 · **Morale** — (never flees) · **Defense** DR14

A bejeweled, bone-dry martyr propped in a forgotten ossuary, animate with a cold and jealous holiness.

- **Withering touch** (DR14 attack): 1d8 damage and the victim ages, suffering −1 (DR +1) to all tests until they rest a full night.
- **Toll of bells** (once per scene): every living creature that hears it makes a DR14 Presence test or is paralyzed by dread for 1d4 rounds.
- **Hoard-bound:** will not leave its reliquary. Steal its gilding and it pursues to the ends of the wretched earth.

bestiary.name: Plague Rat Swarm
bestiary.statblock:
**HP** 10 · **Morale** 4 · **Defense** DR12

A boiling carpet of fat, sore-covered rats that strips a corpse to bone in minutes.

- **Gnawing tide** (auto-hit, no test): 1d4 damage to every creature in its space each round.
- **Disease:** anyone bitten passes DR12 Toughness or becomes **Infected**.
- **Diffuse:** ordinary weapons deal only 1 damage per hit. Fire, flood, and area effects deal full damage.
- **Scatters:** disperses if reduced to 2 HP or driven off by flame.

bestiary.name: Bloodless
bestiary.statblock:
**HP** 11 · **Morale** 9 · **Defense** DR13

White-eyed, parchment-skinned dead that thirst for the warmth of living blood.

- **Draining bite** (DR12 attack): 1d6 damage, and the Bloodless heals half that (rounded up).
- **Mesmerize** (once per target): the victim meets its gaze and tests DR12 Presence or stands transfixed for 1 round.
- **Sun-fearing:** suffers −2 (DR +2) and 1 HP per round in direct sunlight or holy flame.

bestiary.name: Wickhead
bestiary.statblock:
**HP** 9 · **Morale** 7 · **Defense** DR12

A hunched thing whose skull is a guttering tallow candle, dripping and ever-burning.

- **Burning grasp** (DR12 attack): 1d6 fire damage; on a hit the victim risks catching alight (DR12 Agility or take 1d4 more next round).
- **Hateful light:** sheds sickly light in a wide radius; any creature it can see by its own flame is attacked at +2 to hit.
- **Snuffed:** dousing its candle (a called shot, DR16) drops it instantly to 0 HP.

bestiary.name: Grave-Maggot
bestiary.statblock:
**HP** 13 · **Morale** 5 · **Defense** DR11

A pallid, man-thick worm that burrows up through graveyards to feed on the buried and the mourning alike.

- **Swallowing maw** (DR14 attack): 1d8 damage; on a natural-20 critical it swallows a man-sized victim whole (1d6 acid damage per round inside until cut free).
- **Burrow:** moves unseen beneath loose earth and emerges anywhere within a large area.
- **Blind:** hunts by tremor. Standing perfectly still grants Hard (DR16) to its attacks.

bestiary.name: Two-Headed Basilisk
bestiary.statblock:
**HP** 16 · **Morale** 9 · **Defense** DR13

A scaled horror of the wastes, both skulls hissing venom, its gaze a slow poison.

- **Twin bites** (two DR12 attacks per round): 1d6 damage each; a creature struck twice in one round is also envenomed (DR12 Toughness or lose 1d4 HP next round).
- **Petrifying gaze** (once per scene): one creature meeting its eyes tests DR14 Toughness or stiffens, slowed to half actions for 1d4 rounds.
- **Two minds:** unaffected by attacks or Powers that target a single mind.

bestiary.name: Heretical Priest
bestiary.statblock:
**HP** 10 · **Morale** 9 · **Defense** DR12

A robed zealot of a forbidden creed, mouth full of prayers that were never meant to be spoken.

- **Ritual dagger** (DR12 attack): 1d4 damage.
- **Unclean Scroll** (DR14 Presence to invoke): once per scene calls down a blast that deals 1d6 damage to all foes within reach, or summons 1d4 **Pale Ones** from the nearest unhallowed ground.
- **Zealous flock:** rarely travels with fewer than 1d4 fanatic acolytes (treat as Goblin Scavengers with knives).

bestiary.name: Berserker
bestiary.statblock:
**HP** 12 · **Morale** 11 · **Defense** DR11

A howling, foam-flecked killer who has long since traded reason for slaughter.

- **Great axe** (DR12 attack, +1 to hit): 1d10 damage.
- **Frenzy:** when reduced below half HP, attacks twice per round but defends at Easy (DR -2 for its foes).
- **Fearless:** never tests Morale and cannot be Frightened. Fights until destroyed.

bestiary.name: Goblin Warlord
bestiary.statblock:
**HP** 15 · **Morale** 9 · **Defense** DR13

The biggest, meanest goblin in a warren, festooned in stolen teeth and rusted finery.

- **Notched cleaver** (DR12 attack, +2 to hit): 1d8 damage.
- **War-cry:** as an action, grants every goblin within earshot +1 to hit and immunity to Morale for one round.
- **Coward at heart:** if dropped below 5 HP and alone, flees and abandons its underlings without hesitation.

bestiary.name: Bog Mummy
bestiary.statblock:
**HP** 13 · **Morale** — (never flees) · **Defense** DR13

Leathery, tar-black dead dredged up from a drowning mire, dripping and patient.

- **Suffocating grasp** (DR12 attack): 1d6 damage; on a hit, the victim is grappled and begins to drown in mud (DR12 Strength to break free; 1d4 damage each round held).
- **Waterlogged:** immune to drowning and to cold; fire deals only half damage until it dries.
- **Sinking:** can drag a grappled victim down into the bog over three rounds — a slow, certain death.

tables.name: Broken (d4)
entries: Jaw shattered and 1d4 teeth lost. Unconscious for 1 hour, then up at 1 HP.
Fall over weeping, paralyzed by pain. Unconscious for 1d4 rounds, then up at 1d4 HP.
Bleeding & dying. Die in 1d4 rounds unless an ally tends you (DR12 Presence). Survive at 1d4 HP.
Dead. Or as good as. The land takes another.

tables.name: A Wretch's Last Words (d6)
entries: "I should have stayed in the gutter where it was warm."
"The bells. Do you hear the bells?"
"Tell no one how I died. Tell no one I lived."
"There was supposed to be more time."
"The dirt is rising. It is always rising."
Nothing. Only a wet, final cough.

tables.name: A Scroll Discovered (d6)
entries: Sacred: a stuttered psalm that heals 1d6 HP to one touched living thing.
Sacred: a ward of light that staggers the unliving within reach for 1 round.
Unclean: a name that boils the blood — 1d6 damage to one foe you can see.
Unclean: a whispered bargain that opens any one lock or unbinds any one chain.
Unclean: rotted ink that summons 1d4 Pale Ones to serve for a single scene.
Sacred: a blessing that grants one ally +2 to their next test, then crumbles to ash.

rollPresets.name: Agility test (Defend / dodge)
rollPresets.note: Also your Defense roll when something attacks you.

rollPresets.name: Presence test (Powers / aim)
rollPresets.note: Invoke Scrolls; fire a bow; impose your will.

rollPresets.name: Strength test (Attack / force)
rollPresets.note: Swing a melee weapon; break, lift, grapple.

rollPresets.name: Toughness test (Resist)
rollPresets.note: Resist poison, disease, cold, and exhaustion.

rollPresets.name: Melee Attack
rollPresets.note: DR12 + roll ≥ DR. Hit = weapon damage − foe armor.

rollPresets.name: Ranged Attack
rollPresets.note: Bow, sling, crossbow. Range and cover may raise DR.

rollPresets.name: Defend (Dodge)
rollPresets.note: Defend is DR12; armor worn raises the DR (see Damage & Armor).

rollPresets.name: Invoke a Scroll
rollPresets.note: Sacred or Unclean. Fumble = a terrible miscast.

rollPresets.name: Morale (NPC/foe)
rollPresets.note: GM rolls 2d6 ≤ Morale; under = fights on, over = flees.

rollPresets.name: Reaction (NPC)
rollPresets.note: How a stranger first meets you — see Reaction & Morale.

docs.npcStatFormat: AGI / PRE / STR / TGH | HP | Morale | DR(def)

reference.title: Tests & DR
reference.body:
When the outcome is uncertain and failure has teeth, make a **Test**: roll **1d20**, add the relevant **Ability** (Agility, Presence, Strength, or Toughness), and compare the total to the **Difficulty Rating (DR)** the GM has set.

> **Meet or beat the DR → success.** Below it → failure.

The default DR is **12**. The GM raises or lowers it to fit the fiction:

| Difficulty | DR |
|---|---|
| **Easy** | 6 |
| **Standard** | 12 |
| **Hard** | 16 |
| **Nigh Impossible** | 18 |

Rather than tracking long lists of modifiers, simply shift the DR up or down a step for favorable or hostile conditions. **Don't call for a test** when there's no risk and no pressure — just narrate it. The dice are for moments that can cost blood, time, or your wretched soul.

reference.title: Criticals & Fumbles
reference.body:
The 20-sided die has two faces of fate, regardless of the DR:

- **Natural 20 — Critical Success.** The best plausible outcome. In combat, a critical attack deals **double damage** and may shatter a shield or pierce armor. On a skill test, you achieve more than you hoped.
- **Natural 1 — Fumble.** Disaster beyond a simple miss. A weapon breaks or is dropped, a Scroll miscasts horribly, footing is lost, attention is drawn. In combat a fumbling attacker takes **1d4 damage** or leaves themselves open to a free strike.

Naturals override everything: a natural 20 hits no matter how high the DR, and a natural 1 fails no matter how low. Save the fumble's worst consequences for the moments where they'll sting most.

reference.title: Combat & Initiative
reference.body:
Combat is fast, ugly, and frequently fatal.

**Initiative.** At the start of a fight each side rolls **1d6**. On the players' winning roll, the party acts first this round; otherwise the enemies do. Reroll initiative each round, so the tide can turn.

**A turn.** On your turn you may **move** a short distance and take **one action** — attack, invoke a Scroll, drink a potion, flee, or attempt something desperate. A few words are free.

**Attacking** is a player-facing roll: *you* roll to attack when you strike, and *you* roll to Defend when something strikes you. The GM rarely rolls to hit; the dice stay in the players' trembling hands.

**Distance** is abstract — close (melee), nearby (a short dash), or far (a ranged shot). Don't bother with grids unless you crave them.

reference.title: Attack & Defend
reference.body:
**To Attack** a foe, make a **DR12 test**: **Strength** for melee, **Presence** for ranged. Meet or beat DR12 and you hit — roll the weapon's damage and subtract the target's armor.

**To Defend** when a foe attacks *you*, make a **DR12 Agility test**. Succeed and you avoid the blow entirely. Fail and you take the foe's weapon damage, minus your armor.

| Weapon | Damage |
|---|---|
| Femur / knife / sling | 1d4 |
| Sword / axe / bow / mace | 1d6 |
| Crossbow | 1d8 |
| Zweihänder / great axe | 1d10 |

- **Wielding two-handed** weapons needs both hands free; no shield.
- **Out of reach?** A melee Attack against a far foe is impossible; close the distance first.
- **Cover, darkness, or footing** may shift the Attack or Defend DR up a step (to DR16).

reference.title: Damage & Armor
reference.body:
Damage rolled is subtracted from the target's **Hit Points (HP)**. Armor blunts the blow.

| Armor | Reduces damage by | Drawback |
|---|---|---|
| **None** | — | — |
| **Light** (furs, leather) | −1d2 | none |
| **Medium** (chain, brigandine) | −1d4 | DR +2 on Agility & Defend tests; Presence (Scrolls) at DR +2 |
| **Heavy** (plate) | −1d6 | DR +2 on Agility & Defend, cannot run, Scrolls forbidden |

**Shields** let you **break the shield** to negate one hit entirely (the shield is destroyed), or grant a small bonus to Defend at the GM's discretion.

When damage drops you to **0 HP or below**, you are **Broken** (see *Broken & Death*). HP recovers slowly: a night's rest and a meal restores **1d4 HP**; a successful DR12 Presence test to tend wounds can restore **1d6** once per rest.

reference.title: Broken & Death
reference.body:
At **0 HP** you fall, **Broken**. Roll **1d4** on the Broken table:

| d4 | Result |
|---|---|
| **1** | Jaw shattered, lose 1d4 teeth. Unconscious 1 hour, then up at 1 HP. |
| **2** | Collapse weeping in agony. Unconscious 1d4 rounds, then up at 1d4 HP. |
| **3** | **Bleeding out** — dead in 1d4 rounds unless an ally reaches you and passes a DR12 Presence test. Tended, you rise at 1d4 HP. |
| **4** | **Dead.** The land claims you. Roll up a new wretch. |

**Omens** (see *Getting Better*) may be spent to reroll a Broken result or downgrade death to mere ruin. Death in this world is cheap, swift, and rarely heroic — make the survivors remember it.

reference.title: Reaction & Morale
reference.body:
Not every meeting is a fight, and not every fight is to the death.

**Reaction.** When the players meet an NPC or creature whose disposition is unclear, the GM rolls **2d6**:

| 2d6 | Reaction |
|---|---|
| 2 | Hostile — attacks or flees in terror |
| 3–5 | Wary / unfriendly |
| 6–8 | Neutral / uncertain |
| 9–11 | Talkative / open to deals |
| 12 | Helpful (for now) |

**Morale.** When a foe is first bloodied, loses a leader, or sees half its number fall, the GM rolls **2d6** against its **Morale** score. **Roll over Morale → it breaks** and flees, surrenders, or scatters. Roll equal or under → it fights on. Mindless undead and fanatics (Morale —) never check; they fight until destroyed.

reference.title: Powers & Scrolls (Sacred & Unclean)
reference.body:
Magic lives in fragile, dangerous **Scrolls**, never memorized. There are two kinds:

- **Sacred Scrolls** — prayers, wards, and healings of dying faiths. Generally protective.
- **Unclean Scrolls** — names, curses, and summonings best left buried. Generally destructive.

**Invoking** a Scroll is a **Presence test**, usually **Hard (DR16)** for the unclean and **DR12** for the sacred. The number you can attempt per day is limited by your Presence:

| Presence | Powers / day |
|---|---|
| −3 to −1 | 0 |
| ±0 to +1 | Presence value, min 1 |
| +2 to +6 | Presence value |

- **Success:** the Scroll's effect occurs. **Fumble (natural 1):** a miscast — take 1d4 damage, age a year, draw something hungry, or worse.
- **Armor interferes:** medium or heavy armor raises Scroll DR or forbids them entirely (see *Damage & Armor*).
- A used Scroll often crumbles; treasure them like the last warmth on earth.

reference.title: Getting Better (Advancement)
reference.body:
There are no neat experience tables here — only survival, silver, and dwindling fate.

**Surviving a session** is the reward. At the end of each session that your wretch lives through, roll for improvement at the GM's cue:

- **Abilities:** roll **1d6** for each of your four Abilities. On a **5–6**, that Ability rises by **+1** (to a maximum of **+6**); on a **1–2** it may *fall* by 1 in a cruel session.
- **HP:** roll your current **HP die** anew; if it beats your maximum, raise your max HP by the difference.
- **Silver** scavenged buys gear, scrolls, healers, and safe beds — the only real power in a doomed land.

**Omens** are your fate points. You begin each session with **1d2 Omens** and may spend one to: reroll any die, ignore the damage of one attack, lower a Broken result by one step, or detonate a held Scroll for double effect. Spent Omens do not return until the next session.

reference.title: The Calendar & Misery
reference.body:
This is a world that has been promised its end, and the promise is being kept.

**The Calendar of Misery.** The world dies by prophecy, in named stages. The GM may track an ominous **Misery** clock that fills as the campaign grinds on — each filled segment unleashing a foretold calamity: a black sun, a rain of teeth, the rivers running with rot, the dead rising in numbers, the final silence. Players cannot stop it; they can only choose how they meet it.

**Living in the end times.**
- **Cold, hunger, and rot** are constant: a night exposed or a foul meal calls for a DR12 Toughness test or 1d4 HP and a slide toward **Falling Apart**.
- **Hope is a resource to be spent**, not assumed. Small mercies — a fire, a meal, a kindness — are the only true treasure.
- **The dead do not stay down**, and the living are often worse. Trust little; expect betrayal; die well.

Run it grim, run it fast, and let the doom be felt in every roll of the die.

reference.title: Creating a Wretch
reference.body:
Making a doomed soul takes minutes — they rarely last much longer.

1. **Abilities.** Roll **3d6** for each of **Agility, Presence, Strength, Toughness** (in that order) and read the result: 1-4 = **−3**, 5-6 = **−2**, 7-8 = **−1**, 9-12 = **±0**, 13-14 = **+1**, 15-16 = **+2**, 17-18 = **+3**.
2. **Hit Points.** Roll **1d8 + Toughness** (minimum 1).
3. **Omens.** Roll **1d2** — your fate points for the session.
4. **Gear.** A weapon, perhaps scraps of armor, a few coins, and whatever wretched possessions the GM allows. Maybe a Scroll, if the dice are kind.
5. **A name and a grudge.** Why are you still walking when the world is ending? Note it. It is the only thing that is yours.

That's it. Now go and die interestingly.

reference.title: Conditions & Afflictions
reference.body:
The dying world marks its own. The common afflictions:

- **Broken** — at 0 HP you collapse; roll on the Broken table (see *Broken & Death*).
- **Bleeding Out** — dying. Dead in 1d4 rounds unless an ally tends you (DR12 Presence).
- **Infected** — a festering wound or filthy bite. Each day, DR12 Toughness or lose 1d4 HP as the rot spreads. Three failures is a fevered death, and you may not stay dead.
- **Falling Apart** — cursed, starving, or rotting within. −2 (DR +2) to all tests and no HP recovery until the cause is cured.

Most conditions are removed by rest, a healer, clean water, the right Scroll, or a desperate prayer. Some are removed only by death — which, here, is never the end of the story.
